headline: The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
description: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.

description: While Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu has chosen to communicate minimally to avoid appearing nervous, French President Emmanuel Macron has been irritated by the criticism and has insisted that he has done 'a great deal of work.'

headline: European lawmakers call on FIFA to investigate 'Peace Prize' awarded to Trump by Infantino
description: In a letter sent Monday to the president and ethics committee of football's governing body, around 50 MEPs request clarification regarding the trophy awarded to the US President in 2025.

headline: Macron brings back viral aviator sunglasses
description: At a ceremony with the Sultan of Oman, President Emmanuel Macron once again sported his now-iconic aviator sunglasses, which he debuted at the Davos World Economic Forum in January, in a speech featuring the English phrase 'for sure.'

headline: 250 years of American independence: Massacre on the prairie
description: 'America 250' (8/13). While US Army troops and those of the Confederacy fought each other, another conflict was taking place on the plains of the American West, against Native Americans. In November 1864, at Sand Creek, in what became Colorado in 1876, the thirst for gold and land drove settlers to barbarism.

description: France's scientific community faces a host of challenges, from dwindling financial resources, ideological attacks and the emergence of artificial intelligence. The result is worrying for researchers nationwide, at a time when France is also slipping behind in scientific publishing.

headline: 'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
description: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

headline: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
description: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.
